executive_summary: The submitted Business Requirements Document outlines a cloud-based inventory management platform intended to replace a legacy on-premise system used by a mid-sized retail chain with 47 locations. The core functional scope covers real-time stock tracking, automated reorder triggers, supplier integration via EDI, and a role-based reporting dashboard accessible to store managers and corporate buyers. Several non-functional requirements around data residency, audit logging, and 99.9% uptime SLA are specified but lack measurable acceptance thresholds, which will require clarification before development begins. Overall the document provides a solid foundation for sprint planning, though three areas — offline mode behaviour, returns processing workflow, and bulk import validation rules — need additional detail to avoid scope ambiguity during implementation.

user_stories.feature: view real-time stock levels for all SKUs in my location
user_stories.benefit: I can make informed decisions about floor replenishment without waiting for end-of-day reports

user_stories.feature: receive automated low-stock alerts with suggested reorder quantities
user_stories.benefit: I can prevent stockouts across all locations without manually monitoring each store

user_stories.feature: scan incoming shipments and reconcile them against purchase orders
user_stories.benefit: I can confirm receipt accuracy and update inventory counts in real time

user_stories.feature: export inventory valuation reports filtered by date range and location
user_stories.benefit: I can produce accurate COGS figures for monthly close without manual data extraction

user_stories.feature: configure role-based access permissions for each user group
user_stories.benefit: I can ensure staff only access data relevant to their responsibilities and maintain compliance

user_stories.feature: look up product availability at nearby locations from a mobile device
user_stories.benefit: I can direct customers to the nearest store that has the item in stock

acceptance_criteria.given: a store manager is logged in and has selected their location
acceptance_criteria.when: they navigate to the Stock Overview dashboard
acceptance_criteria.then: all SKUs for that location are displayed with quantities updated within the last 60 seconds

acceptance_criteria.given: a SKU's on-hand quantity falls below its configured reorder threshold
acceptance_criteria.when: the inventory service processes the next stock update cycle
acceptance_criteria.then: an alert email is sent to the assigned corporate buyer within 5 minutes containing the SKU, current quantity, and suggested reorder amount

acceptance_criteria.given: a warehouse operator scans a shipment barcode linked to an open purchase order
acceptance_criteria.when: all line items are scanned and quantities are confirmed
acceptance_criteria.then: the purchase order status changes to Received, inventory counts are incremented, and a receipt record is created with a timestamp and operator ID

acceptance_criteria.given: a finance analyst selects a date range and one or more locations
acceptance_criteria.then: a CSV file is downloaded within 10 seconds containing SKU, description, quantity on hand, unit cost, and total value for the selected filters

acceptance_criteria.given: an IT administrator is on the User Management screen
acceptance_criteria.when: they assign a role to a user account and save
acceptance_criteria.then: the user's session permissions are updated immediately and an audit log entry is created recording the change, the administrator's ID, and the timestamp

acceptance_criteria.given: a store associate searches for a product by name or SKU on the mobile app
acceptance_criteria.then: each result shows the quantity available at each location within a 50-mile radius, sorted by distance

gap_flags.description: The BRD specifies offline mode as a supported capability for store associates but provides no detail on which operations must function without connectivity, how long offline data should be retained, or how conflicts are resolved when the device reconnects. Without this, the engineering team cannot scope the sync architecture or estimate effort accurately.

The 99.9% uptime SLA is stated as a non-functional requirement but no measurement window, exclusion periods (e.g. planned maintenance), or monitoring methodology is defined. This makes the SLA unenforceable and creates risk during vendor contract negotiations.

The returns processing workflow is referenced in section 4.3 as in scope but no user stories, process steps, or data requirements are provided for it. Returns directly affect inventory counts and financial reporting, so this gap should be resolved before the first sprint.

Data residency requirements mention that all customer data must remain within the EU, but the BRD does not classify which data fields are considered customer data versus operational data. This ambiguity could affect database schema design, cloud region selection, and GDPR compliance posture.
